content: Please install dependencies in the repo root and run the unit tests.
Attempting to install dependencies. Command: npm install

name: bash
output:
$ npm install
npm WARN old lockfile
npm ERR! code ELOCKVERIFY
npm ERR! ELOCKVERIFY: Lockfile detected from another package manager: pnpm-lock.yaml
npm ERR! npm install cannot proceed safely in a pnpm-managed repo.
npm ERR! Please run: pnpm install

npm ERR! A complete log of this run can be found in:
npm ERR!     /Users/dev/.npm/_logs/2025-01-15T10_22_37_123Z-debug-0.log

content: Correction: we use pnpm in this repository. Please run pnpm install (not npm) and rely on pnpm-lock.yaml. CI uses pnpm install --frozen-lockfile.
Understood. I will use pnpm install for dependency setup going forward.
Feature wish: when errors are long, could you automatically condense the log into a brief summary with key lines and suggested next steps?
Acknowledged. A log summarization helper would improve readability and speed up diagnosis.
